title: US customary conversion word problems
description: Practice US customary length, volume, and mass problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
topic_path: Topic B: Problem solving with measurement

assessment_items.question_content:
Brooklyn purchased $3 \text { pounds}$ of pasta and $12 \text { ounces}$ of cheese to make macaroni and cheese.

**What is the total weight in ounces of the pasta and cheese combined?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{3}\text { pounds}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{3} \text{ pounds}=\greenD{3}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{48}\text{ ounces}$.

Brooklyn also purchased $\maroonD{12} \text{ ounces}$ of cheese.

We can add to find the total number of ounces for the pasta and cheese.

$\blueD{48} + \maroonD{12} = \goldE{60} \text { ounces}$
The total weight of the pasta and cheese combined is  $\goldE{60} \text { ounces}$.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
Noa purchased $\dfrac34 \text { pound}$   of jelly beans and $3 \text { ounces}$ of sour gummies. 

**How many ounces of candy did Noa purchase all together?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{\dfrac34}\text { pound}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{\dfrac34} \text{ pound}=\greenD{\dfrac34}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{12}\text{ ounces}$.

Noa also purchased $\maroonD{3} \text{ ounces}$ of sour gummies.

We can add to find the total number of ounces of candy.

$\blueD{12} + \maroonD{3} = \goldE{15} \text { ounces}$
Noa purchased  $\goldE{15} \text { ounces}$ of candy all together.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A bead shop sells their beads by the pound. A new shipment of beads weighing  $2 \text { pounds}$ is divided into $8$ equal-sized bags.   

**How many ounces of beads are in each bag?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{2}\text { pounds}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{2} \text{ pounds}=\greenD{2}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{32}\text{ ounces}$.

The beads are divided into $\maroonD8$ equal sized bags.

We can divide to find the number of ounces of beads in each bag.

$\blueD{32} \div \maroonD{8} = \goldE{4} \text { ounces}$
There are $\goldE4 \text { ounces}$ of beads in each bag.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A teacher orders $20$ copies of a new graphic novel for his class to read. Each of the books weighs the same amount. The total weight of all the books is $10 \text { pounds}$.

**What is the weight, in ounces, of $1$ book?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{10}\text { pounds}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{10} \text{ pounds}=\greenD{10}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{160}\text{ ounces}$.

Each of the $\maroonD{20}$ books weighs the same amount.

We can divide to find how many ounces each book weighs.

$\blueD{160} \div \maroonD{20} = \goldE{8} \text { ounces}$
Each book weighs $\goldE8 \text { ounces}$.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A box is filled with $20 \text{ pounds}$ of paper.  Brown paper takes up $\dfrac14$ of the box.

**How many ounces does the brown paper weigh?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{20}\text { pounds}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{20} \text{ pounds}=\greenD{20}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{320}\text{ ounces}$.

Brown paper takes up $\dfrac14$ of the box, so we can divide by $\maroonD4$ to find the weight of the brown paper.

$\blueD{320} \div \maroonD{4} = \goldE{80} \text { ounces}$
The brown paper weighs $\goldE{80} \text{ ounces}$.

options.explanation:

- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
Tre is building a fence. He purchased $4 \text { pounds}$ of nails. He used $14 \text { ounces}$ of nails on his first day of building. 

**What is the weight, in ounces, of the remaining nails?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{4}\text { pounds}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

So, $\greenD{4} \text{ pounds}=\greenD{4}\times \purpleD{16}=\blueD{64}\text{ ounces}$.

Tre uses  $\maroonD{14} \text{ ounces}$ of nails the first day.

We can subtract to find the weight of the nails that are remaining.

$\blueD{64} - \maroonD{14} = \goldE{50} \text { ounces}$
There are $\goldE{50} \text { ounces}$ of nails remaining.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||

$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A sunflower grew $4 \text { inches}$ during the first week after it was planted. Then it grew an additional $4 \text { feet}$ over the next month.

**What is the total height of the sunflower in inches?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{4}\text { feet}$ to inches.  

$1 \text{ foot}=\purpleD{12}\text{ inches}$

So, $\greenD{4} \text{ feet}=\greenD{4}\times \purpleD{12}=\blueD{48}\text{ inches}$.

The sunflower grew an additional $\maroonD{4} \text{ inches}$ during the first week.

We can add find out the height of the sunflower.

$\blueD{48} + \maroonD{4} = \goldE{52} \text { inches}$
The total height of the sunflower is $\goldE{52} \text { inches}$.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
David is making a wall out of bricks. Each brick is $\dfrac23 \text{ foot}$ long. There are $20$ bricks lined up end to end to make the wall. 

**What is the total length of the wall in inches?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{\dfrac23}\text { foot}$ to inches.  

$1 \text{ foot}=\purpleD{12}\text{ inches}$

So, $\greenD{\dfrac23} \text{ foot}=\greenD{\dfrac23}\times \purpleD{12}=\blueD{8}\text{ inches}$.

There are $\maroonD{20}$ bricks lined up end to end.

We can multiply to find the total length.

$\blueD{8} \times \maroonD{20} = \goldE{160} \text { inches}$
The total length of the wall is $\goldE{160} \text { inches}$.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A woman takes $12$ equal-sized steps.  She walks a total of $10 \text { yards}$.

**How many inches long is each step?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE{10}\text { yards}$ to inches.  

$1 \text{ yard}=\purpleE{36}\text{ inches}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

So, $\tealE{10} \text{ yards}=\tealE{10}\times \purpleE{36}=\blueE{360}\text{ inches}$.

We can divide to find the number of inches in each of the $\maroonE{12}$ steps.

$\blueE{360} \div \maroonE{12} = \goldE{30} \text{ inches}$
Each step is $\goldE{30} \text { inches}$ long.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A kangaroo takes one hop that is $4 \text { yards }2 \text { feet}$ long.

**How many feet did the kangaroo hop altogether?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{4}\text { yards}$ to feet.  

$1 \text{ yard}=\purpleD{3}\text{ feet}$

So, $\greenD{4} \text{ yards}=\greenD{4}\times \purpleD{3}=\blueD{12}\text{ feet}$.

Next we need to add the additional $\maroonD{2} \text { feet}$.

$\blueD{12} + \maroonD{2} = \goldE{14} \text { feet}$
The kangaroo hopped $\goldE{14} \text { feet}$ all together.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
Jane buys $\dfrac73 \text { yard}$ of fabric to make a cat bed. She starts the project by cutting $4 \text { feet}$ of fabric off the end. 

**How many feet long is the remaining piece of fabric?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{\dfrac73}\text { yard}$ to feet.  

$1 \text{ yard}=\purpleD{3}\text{ feet}$

So, $\greenD{\dfrac73} \text{ yard}=\greenD{\dfrac73}\times \purpleD{3}=\blueD{7}\text{ feet}$.

She cuts off a piece of fabric that is $\maroonD{4} \text {  feet}$ long.

We can subtract to find the number of feet of fabric that remains.

$\blueD{7} - \maroonD{4} = \goldE{3} \text { feet}$
There are $\goldE{3} \text { feet}$ of fabric remaining.

$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|

assessment_items.question_content:
Henry buys $3 \text { gallons }$of ice cream to serve at his birthday party.  He needs $24$ equal-sized dishes of ice cream.

**How many cups of ice cream will be in each dish?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE{3}\text { gallons}$ to cups.  

$1 \text{ gallon}=\purpleE{16}\text{ cups}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

So, $\tealE{3} \text{ gallons}=\tealE{3}\times \purpleE{16}=\blueE{48}\text{ cups}$.

The ice cream will be divided evenly into $\maroonE{24}$ dishes.

We can divide to find the number of cups in each dish.


$\blueE{48} \div \maroonE{24} = \goldE{2} \text { cups}$
Each dish of ice cream will be $\goldE2 \text{ cups}$.

$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A school cafeteria uses $10 \text { quarts}$ of milk to make $8$ bowls of  chocolate pudding.

**How many cups of milk are in each bowl of chocolate pudding?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE{10}\text { quarts}$ to cups.  

$1 \text{ quart}=\purpleE{4}\text{ cups}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

So, $\tealE{10} \text{ quarts}=\tealE{10}\times \purpleE{4}=\blueE{40}\text{ cups}$.

The milk is used to make $\maroonE{8}$ bowls of pudding.

We can divide to find the number of cups in each bowl of pudding.


$\blueE{40} \div \maroonE{8} = \goldE{5} \text { cups}$
There are $\goldE5 \text{ cups}$ of milk in each bowl of pudding.

$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|

assessment_items.question_content:
Lori is making $3$ mugs of hot chocolate. Each mug holds $\dfrac12\text{ pint}$.

**How many cups of hot chocolate does Lori pour?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{\dfrac12}\text { pint}$ to cups.  

$1 \text{ pint}=\purpleD{2}\text{ cups}$

So, $\greenD{\dfrac12} \text{ pint}=\greenD{\dfrac12}\times \purpleD{2}=\blueD{1}\text{ cup}$.

Now, we multiply to find out how much Lori pours in all $\maroonD3$ mugs.

$\blueD{1} \times \maroonD{3} = \goldE{3} \text { cups}$
Lori pours $\goldE{3} \text { cups}$ of hot chocolate altogether.

$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
Ian drinks $3 \text { pints}$ of water during a long hike.   He drinks $1\dfrac{1}{2}\text { cups}$ of water when he gets home. 

**How many cups of water did Ian drink all together?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\greenD{3}\text { pints}$ to cups.  

$1 \text{ pint}=\purpleD{2}\text{ cups}$

So, $\greenD{3} \text{ pints}=\greenD{3}\times \purpleD{2}=\blueD{6}\text{ cups}$.

Ian drinks an additional $\maroonD{1\dfrac12} \text { cups}$ of water when he gets home.

We can add to find the total number of cups of water Ian drank.  



$\blueD{6} + \maroonD{1\dfrac12} = \goldE{7\dfrac12} \text { cups}$
Ian drank $\goldE{7\dfrac12} \text { cups}$ of water all together.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
Blanca buys $2 \text { gallons} $ of green paint. She uses $5 \dfrac{1}{2}\text{ quarts}$ on her front porch and its swing.

**How many quarts of paint does Blanca have left?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE{2}\text { gallons}$ to quarts.  

$1 \text{ gallon}=\purpleE{4}\text{ quarts}$

So, $\tealE{2} \text{ gallons}=\tealE{2}\times \purpleE{4}=\blueE{8}\text{ quarts}$.

Blanca used $\goldE{5\dfrac{1}{2}}\text{ quarts}$ of paint on the porch and swing.

We can subtract to find out how much paint she has left.

$\blueE8 - \goldE{5\dfrac12} = \redE{2\dfrac12} \text{ quarts}$
Blanca has $\redE{2\dfrac12} \text{ quarts}$ of paint left.

$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
